subject: Deterministic health tooling for SKILL.md libraries: inventory, protocol consistency, conflict detection, routing health, and drift detection
grounding_note: This portable skill is grounded in Skill Graph's health-tooling categories and uses whole-file truth sources to avoid brittle line drift while the skill itself continues to evolve.

evals.prompt: A team has 200 SKILL.md files and no automated checks. Which health-tooling categories should they add first?
The manifest generator works on your machine but the sample manifest in the repo is stale. Which class of skill-infrastructure check owns this?
Two skills give opposite instructions for the same target: one says always use a helper and the other says never use it. How should this be handled?
A routing-miss report says users frequently ask for 'tool poisoning' but no skill routes. What should skill-infrastructure recommend?
A skill declares local truth sources but no truth_source_hashes. What state should the drift sentinel report, and what is the fix?
An existing skill has five evals, all with positive expectations. Is that enough for a production skill library?
A batch skill change leaves the working tree dirty, but someone wants to regenerate the manifest and commit it anyway. What does this skill say?
A user asks: 'Scaffold a new SKILL.md for our deploy procedure.' Should skill-infrastructure own that task?
